# daytona_admin_audit_logs (Data Source)

Reads paginated Daytona audit logs across all organizations through the Daytona admin API. This data source requires Daytona admin privileges.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "daytona_admin_audit_logs" "example" {
  limit = 50
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `cursor` (String) Cursor token to request the next page of audit logs.
- `from` (String) Start timestamp in RFC3339 format.
- `limit` (Number) Number of audit log entries to request. Defaults to 100.
- `page` (Number) Page number to request. Defaults to 1.
- `to` (String) End timestamp in RFC3339 format.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Data source identifier.
- `items` (Attributes List) Audit log entries. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items))
- `next_token` (String) Cursor token for the next page, when available.
- `total` (Number) Total matching audit log entries.
- `total_pages` (Number) Total result pages.

<a id="nestedatt--items"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items`

Read-Only:

- `action` (String) Audit action.
- `actor_api_key_prefix` (String) Actor API key prefix, when available.
- `actor_api_key_suffix` (String) Actor API key suffix, when available.
- `actor_email` (String) Actor email address.
- `actor_id` (String) Actor user ID.
- `created_at` (String) Audit log creation timestamp.
- `error_message` (String) Error message, when available.
- `id` (String) Audit log ID.
- `ip_address` (String) Actor IP address.
- `metadata_json` (String) Audit metadata as JSON.
- `organization_id` (String) Organization ID.
- `source` (String) Audit source.
- `status_code` (Number) Resulting status code.
- `target_id` (String) Target object ID.
- `target_type` (String) Target object type.
- `user_agent` (String) Actor user agent.
